noise_top.name: noiseTOP
noise_top.sum: The Noise TOP generates a variety of noise patterns including perlin, simplex, sparse, alligator and random. Some types of noise run on the CPU, while others are calculated on the GPU. The ones that a

constant_top.name: constantTOP
constant_top.sum: The Constant TOP sets the red, green, blue, and alpha (r, g, b, and a) channels individually. It is commonly used to create a solid color TOP image.

composite_top.name: compositeTOP
composite_top.sum: The Composite TOP is a multi-input TOP that will perform a composite operation for each input. Select the composite operation using the Operation parameter on the Composite parameter page.

text_top.name: textTOP
text_top.sum: The Text TOP displays text strings in an image. It allows for multiple fonts, sizes, colors, borders, character separation and line separation. The text can be displayed as bit maps, anti-aliased line

render_top.name: renderTOP
render_top.sum: Renders a 3D scene (Geometry COMPs + Camera COMP + Lights) into a 2D image. The primary bridge between the 3D COMP world and the 2D TOP world.

null_top.name: nullTOP
null_top.sum: The Null TOP has no effect on the image. It is an instance of the TOP connected to its input. The Null TOP is often used when making reference to a TOP network, allowing new TOPs to be added to the ne

switch_top.name: switchTOP
switch_top.sum: The Switch TOP is a multi-input operator which lets you switch which input is passed through using the Input parameter.

select_top.name: selectTOP
select_top.sum: The Select TOP allows you to reference a TOP from any other location in TouchDesigner. To save graphics memory, the Select TOP creates an instance of the TOP references.

feedback_top.name: feedbackTOP
feedback_top.sum: Creates iterative visual feedback effects by re-routing a downstream image back as input each frame. Essential for trails, motion blur, and generative accumulation effects.

glsl_top.name: glslTOP
glsl_top.sum: The GLSL TOP renders a GLSL shader into a TOP image. Use the Info DAT to check for compile errors in your shaders.

blur_top.name: blurTOP
blur_top.sum: The Blur TOP blurs the image with various kernel filters and radii. It can do multi-pass blurs and can do horizontal-only or vertical-only blurs.

level_top.name: levelTOP
level_top.sum: The Level TOP adjusts image contrast, brightness, gamma, black level, color range, quantization, opacity and more. See also the Luma Level TOP which preserves hue and saturation more accurately, but i

ramp_top.name: rampTOP
ramp_top.sum: The Ramp TOP allows you to interactively create vertical, horizontal, radial, and circular ramps. Using the ramp bar and the color picker, you can add as many color tabs to the ramp as you like, each

circle_top.name: circleTOP
circle_top.sum: The Circle TOP can be used to generate circles, ellipses and N-sided polygons.

rectangle_top.name: rectangleTOP
rectangle_top.sum: The Rectangle TOP can be used to generate Rectangles with rounded corners.

cross_top.name: crossTOP
cross_top.sum: The Cross TOP blends between the two input images based on the value of the Cross parameter (refered to as Cross_value below).

edge_top.name: edgeTOP
edge_top.sum: The Edge TOP finds edges in an image and highlights them. For each pixel, it looks at the values at neighboring pixels, and where differences are greater than a threshold, the output's value is higher

flip_top.name: flipTOP
flip_top.sum: The Flip TOP will Flip an image in X and/or Y. It also offers a Flop option to turn each row of pixels into a column.

monochrome_top.name: monochromeTOP
monochrome_top.sum: The Monochrome TOP changes an image to greyscale colors. You can choose from a number of different methods to convert the image to greyscale using the RGB and Alpha menus.

over_top.name: overTOP
over_top.sum: The Over TOP places Input1 'over' Input2. The alpha of Input1 is used to determine what parts of the Input2 image are visible in the result.

transform_top.name: transformTOP
transform_top.sum: The Transform TOP applies 2D transformations to a TOP image like translate, scale, rotate, and multi-repeat tiling. The background can be filled with solid color and alpha.

crop_top.name: cropTOP
crop_top.sum: The Crop TOP crops an image by defining the position of the left, right, bottom, and top edges of the image. The cropped part of the image is discarded, thus reducing the resolution of the image.

cache_top.name: cacheTOP
cache_top.sum: The Cache TOP stores a sequence of images into GPU memory. These cached images can be read by the graphics card much faster than an image cache in main memory or reading images off disk.

in_top.name: inTOP
in_top.sum: The In TOP is used to create a TOP input in a Component. Component inputs are positioned alphanumerically on the left side of the Component.

out_top.name: outTOP
out_top.sum: The Out TOP is used to create a TOP output in a Component. Component outputs are positioned alphanumerically on the right side of the Component.

reorder_top.name: reorderTOP
reorder_top.sum: The Reorder TOP is a multi-input TOP which lets you choose any of the input channels for the R, G, B, and A output. It also gives the option of outputting one, zero or the input luminance to any of th

lookup_top.name: lookupTOP
lookup_top.sum: The Lookup TOP replaces color values in the TOP image connected to its first input with values derived from a lookup table created from its second input or a lookup table created using the CHOP parame

limit_top.name: limitTOP
limit_top.sum: The Limit TOP can limit the pixel values of the input image to fall between a minimum and maximum value, and can quantize the pixels by value or position.

math_top.name: mathTOP
math_top.sum: The Math TOP performs specific mathematical operations on the pixels of the input image.

displace_top.name: displaceTOP
displace_top.sum: The Displace TOP will cause one image to be warped by another image. A pixel of the output image at (Uo,Vo) gets its RGBA value from a different pixel (Ui, Vi) of the Source Image by using the second

fit_top.name: fitTOP
fit_top.sum: The Fit TOP re-sizes its input to the resolution set on the Common Page using the method specified in the Fit parameter menu. This is useful for changing the aspect ratio of an image without distortin

inside_top.name: insideTOP
inside_top.sum: The Inside TOP places Input1 'inside' Input2. The alpha of Input2 is used to determine what parts of the Input1 image are visible.

threshold_top.name: thresholdTOP
threshold_top.sum: The Threshold TOP creates a matte with pixel values set to 0 for pixels below the threshold value, and 1 for pixels greater than or equal to the threshold value.

bloom_top.name: bloomTOP
bloom_top.sum: The Bloom TOP creates a glow effect around bright parts of the input image that simulates light bouncing around a lens assembly of a camera. Parameters control how much of the bright spots of your inp

layout_top.name: layoutTOP
layout_top.sum: The Layout TOP positions multiple input TOPs into rows, columns, or grids. It can either fit all the TOPs into a specific resolution (determined by the Common page) by scaling the inputs, or it can sc

tile_top.name: tileTOP
tile_top.sum: The Tile TOP tiles images in a repeating pattern. It also has a Crop option which crops an image by defining the position of the left, right, bottom, and top edges of the image.

menu: KPretas - Prototype-005 - Windows, Mac, Linux - Unity 6.3 LTS (6000.3.10f1)* <DX11>
(49) 🔴 클로드 이렇게 쓰면 반칙 수준 (실전 팁 공개) - YouTube - Comet

wave_chop.name: waveCHOP
wave_chop.sum: The Wave CHOP makes repeating waves with a variety of shapes. It is by default 10-seconds of 1-second sine waves, a total of 600 frames. You can adjust period (frequency), phase, shape, amplitude and

noise_chop.name: noiseCHOP
noise_chop.sum: The Noise CHOP makes an irregular wave that never repeats, with values approximately in the range -1 to +1.

constant_chop.name: constantCHOP
constant_chop.sum: The Constant CHOP creates new constant-value channels. Each channel can be named and assigned a different value. To create a channel, simply enter a channel name in a name parameter on the Constant pa

math_chop.name: mathCHOP
math_chop.sum: The Math CHOP performs arithmetic operations on channels. The channels of a CHOP can be combined into one channel, and several CHOPs can be combined into one CHOP.

null_chop.name: nullCHOP
null_chop.sum: The Null CHOP is used as a place-holder and does not alter the data coming in.

select_chop.name: selectCHOP
select_chop.sum: The Select CHOP selects and renames channels from other CHOPs of any CHOP network. You can select the channels from control panel gadgets like sliders and buttons. It retrieves channels from one or mo

switch_chop.name: switchCHOP
switch_chop.sum: The Switch CHOP allows you to control the flow of channels through a CHOPnet. It selects one of the input CHOPs by index and copies it exactly. This is useful for selecting one of several "gestures" o

merge_chop.name: mergeCHOP
merge_chop.sum: The Merge CHOP takes multiple inputs and merges them into the output. All the channels of the input appear in the output. The channel order is the channels of the first input followed by the channels

fan_chop.name: fanCHOP
fan_chop.sum: The Fan CHOP converts one channel out to many channels, or converts many channels down to one.

shuffle_chop.name: shuffleCHOP
shuffle_chop.sum: The Shuffle CHOP reorganizes the samples in a set of channels.

trigger_chop.name: triggerCHOP
trigger_chop.sum: The Trigger CHOP starts an audio-style attack/decay/sustain/release (ADSR) envelope to all trigger pulses in the input channels. A trigger point occurs whenever the first input's channel increases acr

count_chop.name: countCHOP
count_chop.sum: The Count CHOP counts the number of times a channel crosses a trigger or release threshold. It operates in either static or realtime ("Cook to Current Frame") mode.

timer_chop.name: timerCHOP
timer_chop.sum: The Timer CHOP is an engine for running timed processes. It outputs channels such as timing fractions, counters, pulses and timer states, and it calls python functions (callbacks) when various timing

pars.l: Exit Segment at End of Cycle

speed_chop.name: speedCHOP
speed_chop.sum: The Speed CHOP converts speed (units per second) to distance (units) over a time range. More generally, you give it a rate (the CHOP input) and it outputs a cumulative value. For example, the Speed CH

slope_chop.name: slopeCHOP
slope_chop.sum: The Slope CHOP calculates the slope (or "derivative" in math-speak) of the input channels.

lag_chop.name: lagCHOP
lag_chop.sum: The Lag CHOP adds lag and overshoot to channels. It can also limit the velocity and acceleration of channels. Lag slows down rapid changes in the input channels. Overshoot amplifies the changes in the

filter_chop.name: filterCHOP
filter_chop.sum: The Filter CHOP smooths or sharpens the input channels. It filters by combining each sample and a range of its neighbor samples to set the new value of that sample. Each filter type uses its own weigh

logic_chop.name: logicCHOP
logic_chop.sum: The Logic CHOP first converts channels of all its input CHOPs into binary (0 = off, 1 = on) channels and then combines the channels using a variety of logic operations.

trail_chop.name: trailCHOP
trail_chop.sum: The Trail CHOP displays a history of its input channels back in time. A window of time is displayed from the current frame back in time, the size of this window is set by the Window Length parameter.

pattern_chop.name: patternCHOP
pattern_chop.sum: The Pattern CHOP generates a sequence of samples in a channel. Unlike the Wave CHOP its purpose is generating arrays of samples that have no reference to time (seconds or frames).

pars.l: Hold Last Value on Empty Buffer

analyze_chop.name: analyzeCHOP
analyze_chop.sum: The Analyze CHOP looks at the values of all the values of a channel, and outputs a single-number result into the output. The output is one sample long. It can analyze for maximum, average, peaks and o

beat_chop.name: beatCHOP
beat_chop.sum: The Beat CHOP generates a variety of ramps, pulses and counters that are timed to the beats per minute and the sync as produced by the Beat Dialog.

clip_chop.name: clipCHOP
clip_chop.sum: License: Only available in TouchDesigner Pro.

copy_chop.name: copyCHOP
copy_chop.sum: The Copy CHOP produces multiple copies of the second input along the timeline of the first input. The first input provides the trigger signals or the convolve levels.

cross_chop.name: crossCHOP
cross_chop.sum: The Cross CHOP is a multi input OP that blends between 2 inputs at a time. This is similar to a Switch CHOP however the Cross CHOP allows for interpolation between the inputs.

cycle_chop.name: cycleCHOP
cycle_chop.sum: The Cycle CHOP creates cycles. It can repeat the channels any number of times before and after the original. It can also make a single cycle have a smooth transition from its end to its beginning, so

delay_chop.name: delayCHOP
delay_chop.sum: The Delay CHOP delays the input. Multiple channels can be fed in to delay each separately. Each channel can have a separate delay time using me.chanIndex.

delete_chop.name: deleteCHOP
delete_chop.sum: The Delete CHOP removes entire channels and/or individual samples of its input.

envelope_chop.name: envelopeCHOP
envelope_chop.sum: The Envelope CHOP outputs the maximum amplitude in the vicinity of each sample of the input. It takes the absolute value of the input, and uses a sliding window of a number of samples to find the maxi

expression_chop.name: expressionCHOP
expression_chop.sum: The Expression CHOP allows you to modify input channels by using math expressions. Each input channel is modified by exactly one expression, and the expressions are looped for multiple channels.

extend_chop.name: extendCHOP
extend_chop.sum: The Extend CHOP only sets the "extend conditions" of a CHOP, which determines what values you get when sampling the CHOP before or after its interval.

function_chop.name: functionCHOP
function_chop.sum: The Function CHOP provides more complicated math functions than found in the Math CHOP : trigonometic functions, logarithmic functions and exponential functions, and also audio decibels (dB)-power-am...

hold_chop.name: holdCHOP
hold_chop.sum: The Hold CHOP waits for a 0 to 1 step on its second input, at which time it reads the current values from the first input (one value per channel). It holds the values constant until it receives anothe

info_chop.name: infoCHOP
info_chop.sum: The Info CHOP gives you extra information about a node. All nodes contain extra inside information, and different types of nodes (TOPs, CHOPs, etc) contain different subsets of information. This addit

interpolate_chop.name: interpolateCHOP
interpolate_chop.sum: The Interpolate CHOP treats its multiple-inputs as keyframes and interpolates between them. The inputs are usually single-frame CHOP channels like those produced by a Constant CHOP. The Interpolate CH

keyframe_chop.name: keyframeCHOP
keyframe_chop.sum: The Keyframe CHOP uses channel and keys data in an Animation COMP and creates channels of samples at a selectable sample rate (frames per second).

limit_chop.name: limitCHOP
limit_chop.sum: The Limit CHOP can limit the values of the input channels to be between a minimum and maximum, and can quantize the input channels in time and/or value such that the value steps over time.

lookup_chop.name: lookupCHOP
lookup_chop.sum: The Lookup CHOP outputs values from a lookup table. The first input (the Index Channel) is an index into the second input (the Lookup Table). Lookup outputs interpolated values from the lookup table.

object_chop.name: objectCHOP
object_chop.sum: The Object CHOP compares two objects and outputs channels containing their raw or relative positions and orientations. The information that can be output is:

parameter_chop.name: parameterCHOP
parameter_chop.sum: The Parameter CHOP gets parameter values, including custom parameters, from all OP types.

rename_chop.name: renameCHOP
rename_chop.sum: The Rename CHOP renames channels. Channels names from the input CHOP are matched using the From pattern, and are renamed to the corresponding name in the To pattern. Channels that do not match the Fro

replace_chop.name: replaceCHOP
replace_chop.sum: The Replace CHOP can be used to replace channels very quickly. The output of channels in Input1 will be replaced by channels found in Input2 if a matching channel exists in Input2. Any channels that a

resample_chop.name: resampleCHOP
resample_chop.sum: The Resample CHOP resamples an input's channels to a new sample rate and/or start/end interval. In all cases, the entire input interval is resampled to match the output interval.

script_chop.name: scriptCHOP
script_chop.sum: The Script CHOP runs a script each time the Script CHOP cooks. By default, the Script CHOP is created with a docked DAT that contains three Python methods: cook, onPulse, and setupParameters. The cook

shift_chop.name: shiftCHOP
shift_chop.sum: The Shift CHOP time-shifts a CHOP, changing the start and end of the CHOP's interval. However, the contents of the channels remain the same.

sort_chop.name: sortCHOP
sort_chop.sum: The Sort CHOP re-orders the inputs channels samples by value or by random. Specifying a channel to be sorted will reorder all channels samples according to the new order. This node does not change the

spring_chop.name: springCHOP
spring_chop.sum: The Spring CHOP creates vibrations influenced by the input channels, as if a mass was attached to a spring.

stretch_chop.name: stretchCHOP
stretch_chop.sum: The Stretch CHOP preserves the shape of channels and the sampling rate, but resamples the channels into a new interval. All data in the range is stretched or compressed to the new start and end positi

trim_chop.name: trimCHOP
trim_chop.sum: The Trim CHOP shortens or lengthens the input's channels. A part of the interval can be preserved or removed. If the channels are being lengthened, the extend conditions of the channel will be used to

sphere_sop.name: sphereSOP
sphere_sop.sum: The Sphere SOP generates spherical objects of different geometry types. It is capable of creating non-uniform scalable spheres of all geometry types.

box_sop.name: boxSOP
box_sop.sum: The Box SOP creates cuboids. These can be used as geometries by themselves, or they can be sub-divided for use with the Lattice SOP.

grid_sop.name: gridSOP
grid_sop.sum: The Grid SOP allows you to create grids and rectangles using polygons, a mesh, Bzier and NURBS surfaces, or multiple lines using open polygons.

noise_sop.name: noiseSOP
noise_sop.sum: The Noise SOP displaces geometry points using noise patterns. It uses the same math as the Noise CHOP.

null_sop.name: nullSOP
null_sop.sum: The Null SOP has no effect on the geometry. It is an instance of the SOP connected to its input. The Null SOP is often used when making reference to a SOP network, allowing new SOPs to be added to the

select_sop.name: selectSOP
select_sop.sum: The Select SOP allows you to reference a SOP from any other location in TouchDesigner. If only one other SOP is referenced, its memory is shared instead of re-created in this SOP.

merge_sop.name: mergeSOP
merge_sop.sum: The Merge SOP merges geometry from multiple SOPs. You can merge SOPs by wiring them into the Merge SOP, and/or you can specify the names in the SOPs parameter, where you can include wildcard Pattern M

transform_sop.name: transformSOP
transform_sop.sum: The Transform SOP translates, rotates and scales the input geometry in "object space" or local to the SOP. The Model Editor and the Transform SOP both work in "object space", and change the X Y Z posi

circle_sop.name: circleSOP
circle_sop.sum: The Circle SOP creates open or closed arcs, circles and ellipses.

line_sop.name: lineSOP
line_sop.sum: The Line SOP creates straight lines.

rectangle_sop.name: rectangleSOP
rectangle_sop.sum: The Rectangle SOP creates a 4-sided polygon. It is a planar surface. The rectangle can be explicitly sized, or sized from other sources such as at the camera's view frustum, or the bounding box of a S

torus_sop.name: torusSOP
torus_sop.sum: The Torus SOP generates complete or specific sections of torus shapes (like a doughnut).

tube_sop.name: tubeSOP
tube_sop.sum: The Tube SOP generates open or closed tubes, cones, or pyramids along the X, Y or Z axes. It outputs as meshes, polygons or simply a tube Primitive.

copy_sop.name: copySOP
copy_sop.sum: The Copy SOP lets you make copies of the geometry of other SOPs and apply a transformation to each copy.

switch_sop.name: switchSOP
switch_sop.sum: The Switch SOP switches between up to 9999 possible inputs. The output of this SOP is specified by the Select Input field. This is useful for switching the output between several inputs depending on t

sort_sop.name: sortSOP
sort_sop.sum: The Sort SOP allows you to sort points and primitives in different ways. Sometimes the primitives are arranged in the desired order, but the point order is not. There are many possible combinations. T

facet_sop.name: facetSOP
facet_sop.sum: The Facet SOP lets you control the smoothness of faceting of a given object. It also lets you consolidate points or surface normals.

divide_sop.name: divideSOP
divide_sop.sum: The Divide SOP divides incoming polygonal geometry. It will smooth input polygons, dividing polygons, as well as sub-divide input polygons using the Bricker option. Bricker is also useful for polygon

convert_sop.name: convertSOP
convert_sop.sum: The Convert SOP converts geometry from one geometry type to another type. Types include polygon, mesh, Bezier patche, particle and sphere primitive.

delete_sop.name: deleteSOP
delete_sop.sum: The Delete SOP deletes input geometry as selected by a group specification or a geometry selection by using either of the three selection options: by entity number, by a bounding volume, and by entity

group_sop.name: groupSOP
group_sop.sum: The Group SOP generates groups of points or primitives according to various criteria and allows you to act upon these groups. Elements can occur in more than one group. Groups are used in many parts o

hole_sop.name: holeSOP
hole_sop.sum: The Hole SOP is for making holes where faces are enclosed, even if they are not in the same plane. It can also remove existing holes from the input geometry.

clip_sop.name: clipSOP
clip_sop.sum: The Clip SOP cuts and creases source geometry with a plane.

extrude_sop.name: extrudeSOP
extrude_sop.sum: The Extrude SOP can be used for:

sweep_sop.name: sweepSOP
sweep_sop.sum: The Sweep SOP sweeps primitives in the Cross-section input along Backbone Source primitive(s), creating ribbon and tube-like shapes. The cross-section primitives are placed at each point of the backbo

skin_sop.name: skinSOP
skin_sop.sum: The Skin SOP takes any number of faces and builds a skin surface over them. If given two or more surfaces, however, the SOP builds four skins, one for each set of boundary curves.

cap_sop.name: capSOP
cap_sop.sum: The Cap SOP is used to close open areas with flat or rounded coverings. Meshes are capped by extending the mesh in either the U or V direction (e.g. a NURBS tube). Faces are capped by appending a sepa

carve_sop.name: carveSOP
carve_sop.sum: The Carve SOP works with any face or surface type, be that polygon, Bzier, or NURBS. It can be used to slice a primitive, cut it into multiple sections, or extract points or cross-sections from it.

pars.l: Cut at All Internal U Breakpoints
Cut at All Internal V Breakpoints

fit_sop.name: fitSOP
fit_sop.sum: The Fit SOP fits a Spline curve to a sequence of points or a Spline surface to an m X n mesh of points.

join_sop.name: joinSOP
join_sop.sum: The Join SOP connects a sequence of faces or surfaces into a single primitive that inherits their attributes. Faces of different types can be joined together, and so can surfaces. Mixed face-surface t

point_sop.name: pointSOP
point_sop.sum: The Point SOP allows you to get right into the geometry and manipulate the position, color, texture coordinates, and normals of the points in the Source, and other attributes. The Point SOP also lets

polyreduce_sop.name: polyreduceSOP
polyreduce_sop.sum: The Polyreduce SOP reduces a high detail polygonal model into one consisting of fewer polygons. The second input's polygons represent feature edges. They are matched to the input mesh by point numbers

project_sop.name: projectSOP
project_sop.sum: The Project SOP creates curves on surface (also known as trim or profile curves) by projecting a 3D face onto a spline surface, much like a light casts a 2D shadow onto a 3D surface. There are two pro

refine_sop.name: refineSOP
refine_sop.sum: The Refine SOP allows you to increase the number of CVs in any NURBS, Bzier, or polygonal surface or face without changing its shape. It is also used to decrease the number of CVs within a given toler

resample_sop.name: resampleSOP
resample_sop.sum: The Resample SOP will resample one or more primitives into even length segments. It only applies to polygons so when presented with a NURBS or Bzier curve input, it first converts it to polygons using

script_sop.name: scriptSOP
script_sop.sum: The Script SOP runs a python script each time the Script SOP cooks. It can create, delete and modify points, primitives and their vertices. It can create custom attributes or built-in attributes like

spring_sop.name: springSOP
spring_sop.sum: The Spring SOP deforms and moves the input geometry using spring "forces" on the edges of polygons and on masses attached to each point.

pars.l: Fixed Points go to Source Positions

subdivide_sop.name: subdivideSOP
subdivide_sop.sum: The Subdivide SOP takes an input polygon surface (which can be piped into one or both inputs), and divides each face to create a smoothed polygon surface using a Catmull-Clark subdivision algorithm. E

texture_sop.name: textureSOP
texture_sop.sum: The Texture SOP assigns texture UV and W coordinates to the Source geometry for use in texture and bump mapping. It generates multi-layers of texture coordinates.

trace_sop.name: traceSOP
trace_sop.sum: The Trace SOP reads an image file and automatically traces it, generating a set of faces around areas exceeding a certain brightness threshold. You can control this threshold and the resolution of the

trail_sop.name: trailSOP
trail_sop.sum: The Trail SOP takes an input SOP and makes a trail of each point of the input SOP over the past several frames, and connects the trails in different ways. It will generate trails of any input geometry

twist_sop.name: twistSOP
twist_sop.sum: The Twist SOP performs non-linear deformations such as bend, linear taper, shear, squash and stretch, taper and twist. Each deformation will distort the object in one or more axes.

cache_sop.name: cacheSOP
cache_sop.sum: The Cache SOP collects its input geometry in a cache for faster random-access playback of multiple SOPs. It should be used when cook times for a chain of SOPs is long and a quicker playback is needed.

add_sop.name: addSOP
add_sop.sum: The Add SOP can both create new Points and Polygons on its own, or it can be used to add Points and Polygons to an existing input.

boolean_sop.name: booleanSOP
boolean_sop.sum: The Boolean SOP takes two closed polygonal sets, A and B. Set these Sources to the SOPs with the 3D shapes that you wish to operate on. There are two important requirements for input geometry:

bridge_sop.name: bridgeSOP
bridge_sop.sum: The Bridge SOP is useful for skinning trimmed surfaces, holes, creating highly controllable joins between arms and body, branches or tube intersections.

creep_sop.name: creepSOP
creep_sop.sum: The Creep SOP lets you deform and animate Source Input (input0) geometry along the surface of the Path Input (input1) geometry.

deform_sop.name: deformSOP
deform_sop.sum: The Deform SOP takes geometry along with point weights (assigned by the Capture SOP) and deforms geometry as Capture Regions are moved. This gives you the flexibility to procedurally modify geometry b

metaball_sop.name: metaballSOP
metaball_sop.sum: The Metaball SOP creates metaballs and meta-superquadric surfaces. Metaballs can be thought of as spherical force fields whose surface is an implicit function defined at any point where the density of

primitive_sop.name: primitiveSOP
primitive_sop.sum: The Primitive SOP is like the Point SOP but manipulates a primitive's position, size, orientation, color, alpha, in addition to primitive-specific attributes, such as reversing primitive normals. The

ray_sop.name: raySOP
ray_sop.sum: The Ray SOP is used to project one surface onto another. Rays are projected from each point of the input geometry in the direction of its normal. This can be used to drape clothes over surfaces, shrin

text_sop.name: textSOP
text_sop.sum: The Text SOP creates text geometry from any TrueType or OpenType font that is installed on the system, or any TrueType/OpenType font file on disk. Unicode is supported.

text_dat.name: textDAT
text_dat.sum: The Text DAT lets you edit free-form, multi-line ASCII text. It is used for scripts, GLSL shaders, notes, XML and other purposes. "Free-form text" is one of the two forms of DATs (the other being tabl

script_dat.name: scriptDAT
script_dat.sum: The Script DAT runs a script each time the DAT cooks and can build/modify the output table based in the optional input tables. The Script DAT is created with a docked (attached) DAT that contains thre

null_dat.name: nullDAT
null_dat.sum: The Null DAT has no effect on the data. It is an instance of the DAT connected to its input. The Null DAT is often used when making reference to a DAT network, allowing new DATs to be added to the net

select_dat.name: selectDAT
select_dat.sum: The Select DAT allows you to fetch a DAT from any other location in the project, and to select any subset of rows and columns if it is a table.

switch_dat.name: switchDAT
switch_dat.sum: The Switch DAT is a multi-input operator which lets you choose which input is output by using the Input parameter.

merge_dat.name: mergeDAT
merge_dat.sum: The Merged DAT is a multi-input DAT which merges the text or tables from the input DATs together.

info_dat.name: infoDAT
info_dat.sum: The Info DAT gives you string information about a node. Only some nodes contain additional string information which can be accessed by the Info DAT.

examine_dat.name: examineDAT
examine_dat.sum: The Examine DAT lets you inspect an operator's python storage, locals, globals, expressions, and extensions.

error_dat.name: errorDAT
error_dat.sum: The Error DAT lists the most recent TouchDesigner errors in its FIFO (first in/first out) table.

fifo_dat.name: fifoDAT
fifo_dat.sum: The FIFO DAT maintains a user-set maximum number of rows in a table. You add rows using the appendRow() method found in DAT Class. When its capacity is reached, the first row is removed. After the max

folder_dat.name: folderDAT
folder_dat.sum: The Folder DAT lists the files and subfolders found in a file system folder and monitors any changes.

insert_dat.name: insertDAT
insert_dat.sum: The Insert DAT allows you to insert a row or column into an existing table.

convert_dat.name: convertDAT
convert_dat.sum: The Convert DAT changes the text format from simple text to table form and vice-versa.

execute_dat.name: executeDAT
execute_dat.sum: The Execute DAT lets you edit scripts and run them based on conditions. It can be executed at the start or end of every frame, or at the start or end of the TouchDesigner process.

evaluate_dat.name: evaluateDAT
evaluate_dat.sum: The Evaluate DAT changes the cells of the incoming DAT using string-editing and math expressions.

parameter_dat.name: parameterDAT
parameter_dat.sum: The Parameter DAT outputs a table of parameter names and values of an operator, including custom parameters, from any OP type.

reorder_dat.name: reorderDAT
reorder_dat.sum: The Reorder DAT allows you to reorder the rows and columns of the input table. You can also use In Specified Order option to get duplicate copies of rows and columns.

substitute_dat.name: substituteDAT
substitute_dat.sum: The Substitute DAT changes the cells of the incoming DAT using pattern matching and substitution strings. It outputs a table with the same number of rows and columns.

transpose_dat.name: transposeDAT
transpose_dat.sum: The Transpose DAT converts rows into columns. The number of rows becomes the number of columns, and vice versa.

base_comp.name: baseCOMP
base_comp.sum: The Base Component has no panel parameters and no 3D object parameters. You would use it for a component that has no panel associated with it, nor any 3D, such as component that converted RGB channels

geometry_comp.name: geometryCOMP
geometry_comp.sum: Wraps SOP geometry for 3D rendering. Provides transforms, material assignment, instancing, and LOD control. Referenced by Render TOP via path parameter, not wire connection.

camera_comp.name: cameraCOMP
camera_comp.sum: Defines the 3D viewpoint and projection for a Render TOP. Controls position, orientation, FOV, near/far clip, and look-at target. Referenced by Render TOP via path parameter.

light_comp.name: lightCOMP
light_comp.sum: The Light Components are objects which cast light into a 3D scene. With the light parameters you can control the color, brightness, and atmosphere of geometry lit by the light. A scene can also be vie

animation_comp.name: animationCOMP
animation_comp.sum: The Animation Component is a special component used for creating keyframe animation channels. The component contains a pre-defined network utilizing a Keyframe CHOP and a number of Table DATs to defin

button_comp.name: buttonCOMP
button_comp.sum: The Button Component is used in panels to provide interactive on/off buttons, including toggle buttons, momentary buttons, and sets of radio buttons or exclusive buttons. Radio and exclusive buttons a

pars.l: Enter as Newline with no Shift

slider_comp.name: sliderCOMP
slider_comp.sum: The Slider Component lets you build sliders in X, Y and XY, and outputs 1 or 2 channels from a Panel CHOP placed in the Slider component.

list_comp.name: listCOMP
list_comp.sum: The List Component lets you create large lists that are highly customizable via the List COMPs initialization and callback functions. Refer to the Build a List COMP article for more info on using this

select_comp.name: selectCOMP
select_comp.sum: The Select component selects a Panel Component from any other location. This allows a panel to appear in multiple other panels. Note that the Select component does not support child panels ie. through

table_comp.name: tableCOMP
table_comp.sum: The Table Component creates a grid of user interface gadgets. These panels are laid out in a grid format where the contents of each cell are defined by DAT tables.

window_comp.name: windowCOMP
window_comp.sum: The Window Component allows you to create and maintain a separate floating or fixed window displaying the contents of any Panel or any Node Viewer.

time_comp.name: timeCOMP
time_comp.sum: The Time Component allows each component to have its own timeline (clock). The Time Component contains a network of operators that can drive a Timeline, drive animations in Animation COMPs, or be used

phong_mat.name: phongMAT
phong_mat.sum: The Phong MAT creates a material using the Phong Shading model. It has support for textures, reflections, bumps, cone lights, rim lights, alpha maps and more. You can output its GLSL shader into two D

pars.l: Write Camera Space Depth to Alpha

pbr_mat.name: pbrMAT
pbr_mat.sum: The PBR MAT creates a material using a Physically Based Rendering (PBR) lighting model. It has support for textures, reflections, bumps, cone lights, rim lights, alpha maps and more.

pars.l: Write Camera Space Depth to Alpha

constant_mat.name: constantMAT
constant_mat.sum: The Constant MAT renders a constant color on a material.

depth_mat.name: depthMAT
depth_mat.sum: The Depth Only MAT can be used to prevent objects from being drawn by making an invisible barrier in Z.

wireframe_mat.name: wireframeMAT
wireframe_mat.sum: The Wireframe MAT renders the edges of polygons and curves as lines.

line_mat.name: lineMAT
line_mat.sum: The Line MAT renders 3D line segments, dots and vectors. The line width and color can be varied based on distance to the camera, using two models: a 1/z dropoff (z = distance from camera), or a near-f

pars.l: Vector Dir Instance Custom Attribute Index
